channel.title: Midtown West on Gourmand Syndrome
channel.description: Recent content in Midtown West on Gourmand Syndrome

item.title: The Polynesian
item.description: Summary This place is located near 42nd Street and 9 ave in the POD hotel. The restaurant decor is kind of interesting as it does give out a tropical-ish vibe to it. There is an outdoor area that has a lot of those basket woven seats with a cushion on them. From where we sat outdoors, we can see views of the H&M building and the Empire State Building in plain sight.

item.title: Kāwi
item.description: Summary We stopped by for lunch for David Chang’s newest Momofuku restaurant and is located in the mall across the street from The Vessel. It’s on the 5th floor near the Bouchon Bakery. Surprisingly, it wasn’t too filled, but then again it’s terribly hot and humid outside. The trading card cocktail consists of plantation pineapple, cruzan blackstrap, smith & cross rums, trader vic’s macadamia liqueur, curry, and yogurt.

item.title: Katsu Hama
item.description: Summary Katsu is some of my favorite Japanese food. We decided to try this restaurant because it was close to where we were in Midtown. This type of food generally is not overly expensive like other Japanese foods, and it’s pretty filling too. And for whatever reason, the pork cutlets are always really good and hearty too. The Berkshire pork cutlet is basically a slightly fattier piece compared to a regular pork cutlet.

item.title: Tonchin
item.description: Summary This place has a pretty decent ambiance. The yuzu margarita was surprisingly pretty good. If you haven’t had yuzu before and like citrus, I recommend you get this if it’s available. I just checked their website’s drink menu, and it’s not on there. This drink wasn’t strong at all, and had a really really good before and after taste. I would drink this over any regular margarita any day.

item.title: Victor's Cafe
item.description: Summary The ambiance and decor of this place is pretty interesting. On Sundays, they have live cuban music playing which is pretty nice. The virgin passion fruit drink was pretty good. It had no alcohol of course, but it came with a sugarcane in it. The juice was not too sweet, and tasted decently fresh. The ceviche de pargo was surprisingly pretty good. I’m normally not a fan of ceviche barring a few places that do it extremely well.

item.title: Thai Noodle House
item.description: Summary I was looking for Wondee Siam but somehow ended up here. Like most Thai places, there are very few that are bad. The kanom jeeb is pretty decent. It’s like fried shrimp shiu mai balls. The country style curry was ordered with Chicken. Despite it having 2 pepper signs on the menu, it’s actually not that spicy. The curry was watery, but it’s easy to consume once you mix the rice with the sauce.

item.title: The Wayfarer
item.description: Summary Stumbled onto this while trying to go to Bobby Vans steak. That place was closed unfortunately. The service here was pretty subpar by New York City standards. The filet mignon was ordered medium rare, but in my opinion it came out medium. The temperature on the steak as a whole came out cold as if it was sitting there for a tad bit too long. The blue cheese sauce was really salty, but perhaps it was there to hide the lack of taste on the steak.

item.title: Emporium Brasil
item.description: Summary This is probably one of my favorite Brazilian spots. The food all in all was pretty good, as well as some of the drinks. Typically, we order caipirinhas. But they had something called batidas. Batidas basically substitute the white sugar for condensed milk. It was interesting and good to me. Every dish comes with a side of rice, beans, salsa, farofa, yucca fried chips, and hot sauce.

item.title: Rice N Beans
item.description: Summary The caipirinhas were decent. As usual I dislike the coconut flavored ones but appreciate the passion fruit ones. The feijoada was the actual star here. It’s much better than some of the other Brazilian places I’ve been to. The meat was plentiful, and the flavor is very good. The bife acebolado was ordered medium rare, and it was cooked medium to medium well.

item.title: Churrascaria Plataforma
item.description: I like this place more than Fogo De Chao. Granted, I have not been to the NY location as of this writing. I’ve been to the LA location twice. The seasoning at this location to me was better. The salad bar was a let down comparably though. It’s small, and they have sushi there which I thought was odd. I still think M-Grill is the best as far as seasoning and char.

item.title: Ipanema
item.description: The food was surprisingly good, and it’s not pricey at all. They give you free sides of rice and beans, so no worries about ordering that.

item.title: Chirp
item.description: The lomo saltado was ok. The sauces cost 60 cents extra.

item.title: Topaz Noodle Bar
item.description: This place was really good for Thai food. Would love to come back again for their Tom Yum noodle soup and their soft shell crab special. Price was not expensive at all either.

item.title: Lady M Cake Boutique
item.description: This was in a small mall, and it was pretty good. We got the eclairs and apple flan.

item.title: Yoon Haeundae Galbi
item.description: Summary The food was actually surprisingly good. I would come back here again for Korean. The galbi was pretty tasty, and I think the price wasn’t too expensive I think. The meat quality is good. I’ve been back here twice already as I enjoyed this more than the other Ktown BBQ joints. Final Verdict I keep recommending this place over other KBBQ places not only because there is less wait but because the food quality is superb.

item.title: Gallagher's Steakhouse
item.description: The cream of spinach tastes like Lawry’s. The horseradish cream was good. The prime rib not as good as Keen’s.

item.title: Keens
item.description: Best prime rib I have ever had. They are known for their mutton chops though, but damn… this prime rib is that good!

item.title: Lobster Press
item.description: This is Marc Forgione’s fast food-ish restaurant near Penn Station. I got the chili lobster here, but it’s not as good as having it at the restaurant. It’s also CLOSED.

item.title: Shinshi Ramen
item.description: Pretty good tsukemen. Get the spicy miso. Tsukemen seems fairly rare in NYC. Still looking for something similar to LA Tsujita.

item.title: Totto Ramen
item.description: I think this is still one of my top favorite ramen places in NYC. It’s cash only.

item.title: Mentoku Ramen
item.description: It seems I only wrote “pretty good” on my spreadsheet, and right now I am drawing a blank about this place.

item.title: Riko Peruvian Cuisine
item.description: So bad. The lomo saltado is really dry. Can’t seem to find a decent lomo saltado in NYC.

item.title: Ootoya
item.description: Pretty good bento boxing place. A little pricey but no need to tip.

item.title: Sake Bar Hagi
item.description: Decent yakitori skewers, but that’s about it. A bit pricey and always a line.

item.title: Kreuther Chocolate
item.description: Across from Bryant Park. Expensive AF chocolate ($3 pp), but the other desserts were amazing. Really good chocolate though.
